calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-19-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 11:10:56 a.m., pausing at 11:28:13 a.m., reconvening at 11:30:07 a.m. and concluding at 11:48:21 a.m.

Plaintiff 777 Equipment Finance LLC represented by Ken I. Ito, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Professional Charter Services, LLC, a California Limited Liability Company not appearing.
Defendant Omar Orozco not appearing.

PATRICIA MATTHEWS (Vice President of Portfolio Management) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court detemines the vehicle in question has been repossessed and sold.
Plaintiff withdraws its request for possession.

Court has reviewed the case and all filings pertaining to this hearing.
Counsel for Plaintiff presents outline, summary of case, an offer of proof, and is examined by the Court.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

SHARED SCREEN VIA ZOOM: Notice of Repossession and Plan to Sale Property dated 03/15/2021

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 5 - Bill of Sale Dated 02/09/2023
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 6 - Involuntary Repossession Costs and Storage Fees
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 7 - Invoice

Court examines Plaintiff's Counsel regarding calculation of attorney fees and Declaration filed on 01/31/2023.

PATRICIA MATTHEWS is examined by the Court. Witness attests that statements made by its counsel are true and correct.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel and its witness, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint. Default entered as to Defendants Professional Charter Services, LLC and Omar Orozco on 12/16/2022. DOES 1-100 were DISMISSED on 12/23/2022. Defendant was personally served on 11/14/2022 as indicated in Proof of Service filed on 12/13/2022. Defendant breached its contractual duty to pay for the purchase of the vehicle and failed to pay on the account.

The vehicle was repossessed on 03/11/2021 and sold on 02/09/2023.
The funds gained from the sale of the vehicle are applied to Defendant's outstanding balance.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff 777 Equipment Finance LLC, and against Defendants Professional Charter Services, LLC, a California Limited Liability Company and Omar Orozco joint and several, as follows:

Plaintiff's requests for commission of sale and pre-judgment interest are DENIED.
Attorney Fees are awarded for default preparation and time for today's hearing.

1. Damages: $260,666.11
2. Attorney Fees: $3,000.00
3. Costs: $769.85

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $264,435.96

Proposed judgment signed as modified.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2023 Continued To Jul-05-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
